title: mitrasacme neglecta
intro: Mitrasacme neglecta, also known as the Desert broom bush or Desert broom, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Mitrasacmeaceae. It is native to Western Australia, and its leaves and stems are traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Mitrasacme neglecta have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as improved wound healing, reduced inflammation, and boosted immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
